annotations.label: Pullback of a smooth function

anchor.section: Pullback of smooth functions and smooth maps

annotations.note: Pullback of a scalar function along a map is plain composition (`f ∘ φ`); Mathlib has no dedicated 'pullback' definition for this.

annotations.label: Pullback of a smooth map

anchor.section: Pullback of smooth functions and smooth maps

annotations.note: The pullback of a map by another map is just precomposition; Mathlib does not introduce it as a named pullback operation.

annotations.label: Pullback bundle

anchor.snippet: is a vector bundle (or indeed any fiber bundle )

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the pullback fiber bundle (`FiberBundle.pullback` / notation `f *ᵖ E`) and the C^n vector-bundle instance `ContMDiffVectorBundle.pullback`.

annotations.label: Pullback of a section

anchor.snippet: precomposition defines a pullback operation on sections

annotations.note: Although the pullback bundle exists in Mathlib, the induced pullback of sections (precomposition into the pullback total space) is not given as a named declaration.

annotations.label: Pullback of a multilinear form

anchor.snippet: is a multilinear form on V defined by precomposing F with Φ

annotations.note: Precomposing a continuous multilinear/alternating form by a linear map is `compContinuousLinearMap` (alternating version in `Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Module.Alternating.Basic`).

annotations.label: Pullback as linear map between dual spaces

anchor.snippet: pullback by Φ defines a linear map between dual spaces

annotations.note: Pullback by a continuous linear map is itself continuous-linear, packaged as `compContinuousLinearMapCLM` (and the multilinear analogue `compContinuousLinearMapL`).

annotations.label: Pushforward of tensors

anchor.snippet: instead there is a pushforward operation

annotations.note: Grep finds no pushforward operation on (multi)linear forms / tensors in the differential-geometric sense in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pullback via inverse pushforward

anchor.snippet: if Φ is invertible, pullback can be defined using pushforward by the inverse function

annotations.note: Mathlib's vector-field pullback `VectorField.mpullback I I' f V x := (mfderiv I I' f x).inverse (V (f x))` realises exactly the 'inverse-pushforward' definition (with the junk value 0 when the differential is not invertible).

annotations.label: Differential of a smooth map
annotations.note: The fibrewise differential between tangent spaces is `mfderiv` (with `mfderivWithin` for the within-a-set variant).

annotations.label: Pullback of a 1-form

anchor.snippet: Applying the above bundle map (pointwise) to this section yields the pullback

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated `pullback` of 1-forms on manifolds; the operation appears only implicitly as `(ω (f x)).comp (fderiv 𝕜 f x)` in the differential-form pullback theorems.

annotations.label: Pullback of a covariant tensor field

anchor.snippet: the pullback of multilinear forms can be combined with the pullback of sections to yield a pullback

annotations.note: No general pullback of covariant tensor fields is defined in Mathlib; only the alternating (differential-form) case appears in inline form.

annotations.label: Pullback of a differential form
annotations.note: Mathlib does not name `pullback` for differential forms; the pullback is written inline as `fun x ↦ (ω (f x)).compContinuousLinearMap (fderiv 𝕜 f x)` (e.g. in `extDeriv_pullback`).

annotations.label: Pullback is compatible with the wedge product

anchor.snippet: It is compatible with the wedge product

annotations.note: Searches for pullback/compContinuousLinearMap interacting with the wedge product return no Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Pullback commutes with the exterior derivative

anchor.snippet: It is compatible with the exterior derivative

annotations.note: `extDeriv_pullback` (with the within-a-set version `extDerivWithin_pullback`) states `d(f*ω) = f*(dω)` in the normed-space form.

annotations.label: Pullback by a diffeomorphism
annotations.note: Mathlib's `Diffeomorph` file does not introduce a pullback (of forms or tensor fields) along a diffeomorphism as a named operation.

annotations.label: Pullback action by an automorphism

anchor.snippet: This induces a pullback action on sections

annotations.note: No diffeomorphism-group pullback action on sections of an associated bundle is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lie derivative via pullback

anchor.snippet: a notion of Lie derivative on any associated bundle is obtained

annotations.note: Mathlib has only Lie-algebra `LieDerivation`; the geometric Lie derivative of sections via pullback of flows is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pullback connection

anchor.snippet: then there is a pullback connection

annotations.note: No connection / covariant-derivative pullback is defined in Mathlib (no general connection framework on manifolds exists yet).
